summary_one_line: Input must encode an IPv6 fragment header that drives reassembly so HandleDatagram is re-entered with aIsReassembled=true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed IPv6 datagram whose next-header/extension-header chain encodes a Fragment header, so the harness path runs HandleFragment + UpdateReassemblyList and re-delivers the reassembled datagram into HandleDatagram with aIsReassembled=true (the blocked true side at ip6.cpp:1245).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds open with a coherent IPv6 header (offset 0x00=0x78, 0x01=0x66, 0x02=0x10) and exercise the reassembly path: HandleFragment runs 19 times and UpdateReassemblyList 10 times for W while both are 0 for L, so the winner actually constructs fragments that get reassembled and re-fed with aIsReassembled=true.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds open with different leading bytes (offset 0x00 is db/dc, 0x01=0x62) and never reach the reassembly functions (HandleFragment W=19 L=0, UpdateReassemblyList W=10 L=0); instead L's traffic stays on the forward/unicast path (ShouldForwardToThread W=0 L=28), so aIsReassembled is always false and the true side never fires (L1245 T=0 F=139).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact next-header / fragment-protocol magic byte values that the header-field comparisons demand, letting cmplog assemble the fragment-extension-header structure that routes through reassembly; naive has only edge-counter feedback and the havoc/token stack with no CMP introspection, so it cannot synthesize those magic field values and stalls on the forward path.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the true branch of `if (aIsReassembled)` at ip6.cpp:1245.
cmplog resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in 8, a decisive I2S pair.
The reassembly functions run only for the winner: HandleFragment 19 vs 0, UpdateReassemblyList 10 vs 0.
UpdateReassemblyList is exercised 10 times by W and never by L, confirming the reassembly path is winner-only.
At the blocker line the true side fires only for W (T=10) and never for L (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1245  T=10 F=102  T=0 F=139  if (aIsReassembled)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser instead stays on the forward path: ShouldForwardToThread runs 28 times for L and 0 for W.
Winner seeds begin with coherent IPv6 header bytes (0x00=0x78, 0x01=0x66, 0x02=0x10) distinct from loser seeds.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one CMP operand at matching input offsets, unlike naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability, only the havoc/token stack with edge-counter feedback.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 13335 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0131a107da449e69`; the 21-level ancestor chain contains 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 20, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose input cannot encode a fragment header (no reassembly path) where cmplog still resolves the aIsReassembled=true side significantly more than naive, showing the divergence is not gated by I2S-driven fragment-field synthesis.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows winner/loser header bytes differ but does not directly pin a specific fragment-header next-header magic value to an I2S CMP operand, so the link from I2S substitution to the reassembly trigger is inferred from path hit-counts rather than a labeled operand.
